title: Techniques for channel estimation
abstract: Techniques are described herein for performing channel estimation for both uplink and downlink channels. Additional reference signals may be allocated or assigned to various resource elements (REs) of a transmission time interval (TTI). The receiving device (e.g., a base station or a user equipment (UE)) may be configured to use the additional reference signals during channel estimation. The use of additional reference signals may improve the accuracy of the channel estimations. In downlink communications, a base station may allocate one or more channel state information reference signals (CSI-RSs) to a port of a UE. In uplink communications, a UE may transmit several sounding reference signals (SRSs) in a group.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1 A method for wi rel ess comm uni cation, compri sing : allocating a plurality of channel state information reference signal (CSI-RS) resource elements (REs) of a transmission time inters al (XT!) to a port of a user equipment (UE); transmitting, to the UE, an indication of the plurality of CSI-RS REs allocated to the port of the UE; and transmitting at least one CSI-RS to the UE during the TTi. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein allocating the CSI-RS REs to the UE comprises: allocating the CSI-RS REs in the TTI such that a ratio of the CSI-RS REs and a number of ports of the UE is greater than one-to-one. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein allocating the CSI-RS REs to the UE comprises: allocating at least two CSI-RS REs of the plurality of CSI-RS REs to the port of the UE in different symbols of the TTI 4 Tire method of claim 1 , wherein allocating the CSI-RS REs to the UE comprises: allocating at least two CSI-RS REs of the plurality of CSI-RS REs to the port of the UE in different radio frequency spectrum bands of the TTI within the same resource block. 5 Tire method of claim 4, wherein allocating the CSI-RS REs to the UE comprises: allocating at least two CSI-RS REs of the plurality of CSI-RS REs to tire port of the UE in different symbols of the TTI. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising: 

 identifying that the UE is configured with ultra-reliable low-latency communications (URLLC) information, wherein allocating the plurality of CSI-RS REs to the port of the UE is based at least in part on identifying that the UE is configured with URLLC information. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising: determining the CSI-RS REs in the TTI based at least in part on a preconfigured mapping, wherein allocating the CSI-RS REs to the UE is based at least in part on determining the CSI-RS REs in the TTI. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising: identifying a channel estimation accuracy for a communication with the UE, wherein allocating the plurality of CSI-RS REs to the port of the UE is based at least in part on identifying the channel estimation accuracy. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: identifying a channel condition of a communication link with the UE, wherein allocating the plurality of CSI-RS REs to the port of the UE is based at least in part on identifying the channel condition. 10. A method for wireless communication, comprising: identifying, by a user equipment (UE), an allocation of a plurality of channel state information reference signal (CSI-RS) resource elements (REs) of a transmission time interval (TTI) to a port of the UE; performing a channel estimation using the plurality of CSI-RS REs allocated to the port of the UE based at least in part on identifying the allocation of the plurality CSI- RS RE s to the port of the UE; and transmitting a channel state information (CSI) report to a base station based at least in part on performing the channel estimation. 11. The method of claim 10, further compri sing: iden tifying that at least two CSI-RS REs of the plurality of CSI-RS REs allocated to the port of the UE are in different symbols of the TTI, wherein performing the channel estimation is based at least in part on identifying that the at least two CSI-RS REs are in different symbols of the TTI. 12. The method of claim 10, further comprising: identifying that at least two CS1-RS REs of the plurality of CS1-RS REs allocated to the port of the UE are in different radio frequency spectrum bands of the TTÎ, wherein performing the channel estimation is based at least in part on identifying that the at least two CS1-RS REs are in different radio frequency spectrum bands of the TTL 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: identifying that the at least two CSI-RS REs allocated to the port of the UE are in different symbols of the TTI, wherein performing the channel estimation is based at least in part on identifying that the at least two CSI-RS REs are in different symbols of the TTI. 14. Tire method of claim 10, wherein a ratio of CSI-RS REs in the TTI allocated to the UE and a number of ports of the UE is greater than one-to-one. 15. The method of claim 10, further comprising: receiving an indication from the base station of the plurality of CSI-RS REs allocated to the port of the UE, wherein identifying the allocation of the plurality of CSI-RS REs to the port of the UE is based at least in part on receiving the indication. 16. The method of claim 10, further comprising: receiving at least one CSI-RS from the base station during the TÎ, wherein performing the channel estimation is based at least in part on receiving the at least one CSI- RS. 17. A method for wireless communication, comprising: determining a plurality of sounding reference signals (SRSs) for transmitting during a transmission time interval (TTI); assigning a plurality of symbols of the TTI for transmitting the plurality of SRSs based at least in part on determining the plurality of SRSs; and transmitting the plurality of SRSs in the TTI based at least in part on assigning the plurality of symbols. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein assigning the plurality of symbols of the TTI comprises: 

 assigning the plurality of symbols of the TTÎ on a short TTI (sTTI)-by~sTH basis. 19. The method of claim 17, further comprising: generating a repetition window indicating a number of symbols to use to transmit the plurality of SRSs, wherein assigning the plurality of symbols is based at least part on the repetition window . 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the repetition window comprises a first portion of symbols in a first subframe and a second portion of symbols in a second subframe, and wherein transmitting the plurality 7 of SRSs comprises: transmitting the plurality of SRSs in a plurality of subframes that comprise the first subframe and the second subframe. 21. The method of claim 17, wherein the plurality 7 of SRSs are associated w ith a fi rst channel for ultra-reliable low-latency communications (URLLC), the first channel having a first channel length. 22. The method of claim 21, further comprising: identifying a second channel having a second channel length longer than the first channel length, wherein the second channel including information for transmitting using at least some of the same symbols as the plurality of SR 8s: and determining at least some content of the information for transmitting using the second channel, wherein transmitting the plurality of SRSs is based at least in part on determining the at least some content of the information to be transmitted using the second channel. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the plurality of SRSs are transmitted based at least in part on the content of the second channel being exclusive of an acknowledgement or a negative acknowledgement of a hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ), a demodulation reference signal (DMRS), or a combination thereof. 24. The method of claim 21, further comprising: 

 identifying a second channel having a second channel length equal to the first channel length, the second channel including information to be transmited using at some of the same symbols as the plurality of SRSs; applying one or more power scaling rules to the plurality of SRSs based at least in least in part on identifying that the second channel has the second channel length equal to the first channel length, wherein transmitting the plurality of SRSs is based at least in part on applying the one or more power scaling rules; and determining at least some content of the information to be transmitted using the second channel, wherein transmitting the plurality of SRSs is based at least part on determining the at least some content of the information to be transmitted using the second channel. 25. The method of claim 24, further comprising: applying the one or more power scaling rules to the plurality of SRSs is based at least in part on the content of the second channel including an acknowledgement or a negative acknowledgement of a hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ), a demodulation reference signal (DMRS), or a combination thereof. 26. The method of claim 17, further comprising: receiving downlink control information from a base station, wherein determining to transmit the plurality of SRSs is based at least in part on receiving the downlink control information. 27. The method of claim 17, further comprising: determining a number of transmissions of the plurality of SRSs in the TÎÎ, wherein assigning the plurality' of symbols is based at least in part on determining the number of transmissions. 28. A method for wireless communication, comprising: receiving, by a base station, a plurality' of sounding reference signals (SRSs) from a user equipment (UE) during a transmission time interval (TTI); performing a channel estimation using the plurality of SRSs received from the UE; and 

 allocating resources to the UE based at least in part on performing the channel estimation. 29. The method of claim 28, further comprising: receiving a first set of SRSs of the plurality of SRSs in a first subframe and a second set of SRSs of the plurality of SRSs in a second subframe, wherein performing the channel estimation is based at least in part on the first set of SRSs in the first subframe and the second set of SRSs the second subframe. 30. The method of claim 28, further comprising: transmitting downlink control information to the UE, wherein determining the plurality of SRSs for transmitting is based at least in part on receiving the downlink control information.
